meaning: n.大肆宣传,大吹大擂,喧噪v.大肆宣传,大吹大擂
examples: Jeff Blyskal, associate editor of Consumer Reports magazine, says the promotional ballyhoo over alliances is much a do about nothing.
They announced, amid much ballyhoo, that they had made a breakthrough.
Many candidates ballyhoo their campaigning in the street.
In that sense, Monday's Premier League match between Manchester City and Manchester United needs no further ballyhoo.
It seems a lot of ballyhoo for a couple of mutts, but William (Bill) Remkus is appropriately solemn.
